description_long: This object is a pair of classic CRT computer monitors, each with a boxy, beige plastic casing and a prominent, thick bezel surrounding the display area. The front of one monitor shows a dark, glassy screen, while the other displays a blue error screen reminiscent of the Windows 'Blue Screen of Death.' The monitors have a deep, angular profile with vent-like ridges on the top and a protruding section at the lower front, likely for control buttons. Both units rest on wide, flat pedestal stands that provide stability. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible branding or decorative elements, and the surfaces appear smooth and slightly reflective.
description: A pair of beige CRT computer monitors with thick bezels, glassy screens, and wide pedestal stands. One screen is dark, the other shows a blue error message.
description_view_0: Side view showing the deep, angular profile and vent-like ridges on the top of the monitors.
description_view_1: Rear view displaying the boxy backs and the wide, flat pedestal stands of both monitors.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, highlighting the depth and angularity of the monitors.
description_view_3: Front view showing both screens: one is dark and glassy, the other displays a blue error message, with thick bezels framing the displays.
attribute_screen_bezel_design: The bezel is thick, flat, and made of beige plastic, with sharp corners and no visible decorative elements or color contrast.
attribute_stand_type: Wide, flat pedestal base made of matching beige plastic, rectangular in shape, providing stable support with no visible adjustability.
attribute_button_placement: A row of buttons is implied on the lower front protruding section beneath the screen, grouped together and easily accessible.
